skill.name: pymc-bayesian-modeling
skill.description: Build and validate Bayesian models with PyMC, including hierarchical models, MCMC sampling, variational inference, diagnostics, and model comparison.
skill.summary: Guide PyMC Bayesian modeling workflows from prior design through diagnostics and comparison.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the external command, weak crypto, certificate, SAM, and reconnaissance matches are false positives from Markdown code formatting and Bayesian terminology. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found. A medium warning remains because the workflow reference demonstrates pickle save and load patterns that can be unsafe with untrusted files.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe Pickle Deserialization Guidance
medium_findings.description: The workflow reference shows saving and loading a model with pickle. This is legitimate for trusted local files, but pickle.load can execute code if a user loads a malicious file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly demonstrate pickle.dump and pickle.load. The risk depends on whether the file is trusted, so the confidence is high for the pattern but not for malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Command Matches
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code and fenced Python examples, not shell execution. No subprocess, os.system, or command execution API was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation formatting around PyMC symbols and code blocks. Semantic review found no actual shell invocation path.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Crypto, SAM, Key, and Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The reported high and critical labels map to ordinary Bayesian text such as Bayesian modeling, sampling, ESS, autoregressive distributions, and dict keys. These are domain terms and Python collection operations, not malware indicators.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain statistical terminology or dictionary key access. There is no certificate material, Windows SAM access, cryptographic use, or network scanning behavior.

low_findings.title: Local Artifact Writes Require User Awareness
low_findings.description: The templates write NetCDF, CSV, and plot files to local paths. This is expected for model analysis, but users should run templates in a project directory they control.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are direct and easy to confirm. They are normal analysis outputs, so the security impact is low when paths are controlled by the user.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pickle Load Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The reference includes a pickle load example. Loading pickle data from untrusted sources can execute arbitrary code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present and well-known as unsafe for untrusted input. The file is documentation, not an active malicious loader.

content.value_statement: Bayesian modeling can be difficult to structure, validate, and explain. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical PyMC workflows, templates, diagnostics, and model comparison guidance.
actual_capabilities: Build PyMC model structures for linear, logistic, hierarchical, count, and time series problems.
Choose priors and likelihoods using distribution reference material.
Run Bayesian workflow steps for prior checks, sampling, diagnostics, and posterior prediction.
Interpret convergence diagnostics such as R-hat, ESS, divergences, and Pareto-k warnings.
Compare fitted models using LOO, WAIC, model weights, and cross-validation guidance.
Use provided Python templates for regression, hierarchical modeling, diagnostics, and comparison.
limitations: It does not install PyMC, ArviZ, Graphviz, or scientific Python dependencies.
It cannot verify model correctness without user data and domain assumptions.
Large models may require substantial compute time and careful sampler tuning.
The included examples may need edits for current PyMC API changes and local data formats.

use_cases.title: Create a First Bayesian Regression
use_cases.description: Set up a PyMC regression model with priors, standardized predictors, sampling, diagnostics, and posterior predictions.

use_cases.title: Analyze Grouped Data
use_cases.description: Design hierarchical models with non-centered parameterization and compare group-level uncertainty.

use_cases.title: Compare Candidate Models
use_cases.description: Use ArviZ LOO, WAIC, model weights, and reliability checks to choose among fitted Bayesian models.
use_cases.target_user: Machine learning engineer evaluating model families

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic PyMC Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a regression task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PyMC Bayesian modeling skill to design a simple regression workflow for my dataset. Include priors, sampling settings, diagnostics, and posterior prediction steps.

prompt_templates.title: Select Priors and Likelihoods
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the distribution reference to recommend priors and likelihoods for these variables and outcomes. Explain the assumptions and alternatives for each choice.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Sampling Problems
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate troubleshooting after fitting a model.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these PyMC diagnostics and propose fixes. Focus on R-hat, ESS, divergences, tree depth, prior scale, and centered versus non-centered parameterization.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Advanced Models
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the model comparison guidance to compare these fitted PyMC models. Interpret LOO or WAIC results, Pareto-k warnings, model weights, and next validation steps.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a Bayesian regression plan for continuous outcomes.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a structured workflow with data scaling, weakly informative priors, NUTS sampling settings, diagnostics, posterior checks, and prediction reporting.

output_examples.input: A user reports divergences in a hierarchical model.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies likely geometry issues and recommends non-centered parameterization, higher target acceptance, predictor scaling, and prior predictive review.

output_examples.input: A user wants to choose between several fitted models.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains LOO or WAIC comparison, checks reliability warnings, discusses uncertainty in rankings, and suggests validation when differences are small.

best_practices: Run prior predictive checks before fitting and revise priors that imply unrealistic data.
Use named dimensions and standardized predictors to improve model clarity and sampling behavior.
Report uncertainty intervals, convergence diagnostics, posterior checks, and model assumptions together.
anti_patterns: Do not interpret posterior summaries before checking convergence, divergences, and effective sample size.
Do not use flat or overly broad priors without checking their prior predictive consequences.
Do not compare models with LOO or WAIC when log likelihood data is missing or reliability warnings are ignored.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build, validate, diagnose, and compare Bayesian models in PyMC using practical workflow guidance.

faq.question: Does it include ready-to-use code templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes templates for linear regression, hierarchical models, diagnostics, and model comparison utilities.

faq.question: Can it choose priors for my data?
faq.answer: It can recommend candidate priors and checks, but final choices need domain knowledge and prior predictive validation.

faq.question: Does it run the model for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and templates that you run in your own Python environment.

faq.question: Which diagnostics does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers R-hat, ESS, divergences, energy diagnostics, trace plots, posterior predictive checks, and Pareto-k warnings.

faq.question: Is it safe to use?
faq.answer: It is suitable for publication with a warning. Review pickle examples and only load files from trusted sources.
